channel.title: Linux on just a note
channel.description: Recent content in Linux on just a note

item.title: Basic KaTeX Math Guide
item.description: Basic KaTeX Math Guide Add math: true to your front matter to enable math. Inline Math Wrap with $: $E = mc^2$ $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$ $\frac{1}{2}$ Display Math Wrap with $$: $$ \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a} $$ Fractions $\frac{1}{2}$ inline $$ \frac{n!}{k!(n-k)!} $$ Exponents $x^2$ and $x^{2n}$ $$ e^{i\pi} + 1 = 0 $$ Subscripts $x_1$ and $x_{ij}$ $$ \sum_{i=1}^{n} i = \frac{n(n+1)}{2} $$ Roots $\sqrt{x}$ and $\sqrt[3]{x}$

item.title: Setting up zram in Artix Linux
item.description: Here is the step for configuring zram in Artix Linux Create /etc/modules-load.d/zram.conf zram Create /etc/modprobe.d/zram.conf options zram num_devices=1 Create /etc/udev/rules.d/10-zram.rules bash ACTION=="add", KERNEL=="zram0", ATTR{initstate}=="0", ATTR{comp_algorithm}="zstd", ATTR{disksize}="4G", RUN="/usr/bin/mkswap -U clear %N" Finally, add zram to fstab for automount Edit /etc/fstab /dev/zram0 none swap defaults,discard,pri=100 0 0 Reboot to take the effect immediately After reboot, Check output with zramctl Voila!! Reference link : https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/ZRAM https://wiki.gentoo.org/wiki/Zram

item.title: My NixOS installation notes
item.description: Just some notes :D Lets go to the plan for the whole system structure :D Partition Filesystem Mountpoint Subvolume Compress (level) /dev/sda1 FAT32 /boot/efi - - /dev/sda2 btrfs / root, home zstd (1:5) Create root partition mkfs.btrfs /dev/sda2 Mount the root partition mount /dev/sda2 /mnt Create btrfs subvolume ( home and root) btrfs create subvolume /mnt/home btrfs create subvolume /mnt/root umount /mnt Create mountpoint and mount the partition respectively mkdir /mnt/boot mkdir /mnt/home mount -o compress=zstd,subvol=root /dev/sda2 /mnt mount -o compress=zstd,subvol=home /dev/sda2 /mnt/home mount /dev/sda1 /mnt/boot Generate the config and copy it to the root partition
